zh.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver於大約1252年出生在英國英格蘭爾。她於1273年與Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum在英國爾阿爾德福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver于大约1252年出生在英国英格兰爾。她于1273年与Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum在英国爾阿爾德福德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Weaver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from an agent derivative of Middle English weven ‘to weave’ (Old English wefan).

English: habitational name from a place on the Weaver river in Cheshire, now called Weaver Hall but recorded simply as Weuere in the 13th and 14th centuries. The river name is from Old English wēfer(e) ‘winding stream’.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘weaver’, for example German Weber , Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) Tkacz or Tkach , Hungarian Takács (see Takacs ), and Slovenian Tkalec, Tekavec or Veber .

variants.name: Weber
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a weaver, Middle High German wëber, German Weber, an agent derivative of weben ‘to weave’. This form of the surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, and the Netherlands. In Hungary it is mostly spelled Wéber and Véber. In Russia, Slovenia, Czechia, Slovakia, and Croatia it is (also) established in the Slavicized form Veber , which is in Czechia also spelled Vebr. Compare Waber , Weaber , Weaver , Webber , and Webre .

History: As a name of ultimately Swiss German origin the surname Weber is also established among the American Mennonites. However, descendants of the early Mennonite settlers, who came to PA in the early 18th century, mostly bear the Americanized form of the name (see Weaver ).

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver was born about 1252, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum in 1273, in Aldford, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver est née environ 1252, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum en 1273, à Aldford, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver wurde um 1252 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1273 in Aldford, Cheshire, England Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver è nata il circa 1252 nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum nel 1273, nel Aldford, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia.

ja.Mondrum, Cheshire, England: イギリスイギリスチェシュアイア
ja.Aldford, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアアルドフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaverは約1252年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアで生まれた。1273年にイギリスチェシュアイアアルドフォードでRichard de Weever Forester of Mondrumと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver(은)는 대략 1252년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1273년 영국 체실르 알드퍼드에서 Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver nasceu aproximadamente 1252, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum em 1273, em Aldford, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver родилась около 1252 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum в 1273 г. в местности алдфорд, чешир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Richard De Weaver nació aproximadamente 1252, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Richard de Weever Forester of Mondrum en 1273, en Aldford, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
